findings: swarm-state.json example uses task_id equal to commander_id, suggesting a copy-paste schema error.
The commander contract lacks a stop/teardown/recovery protocol or heartbeat-response contract.
launch_requested is required before every child launch, but its event shape is not documented in the agent contract examples.
Depth Guard wording is ambiguous because squad leads are shown as general-purpose while workers are restricted to explore/task.
No squad-lead-specific depth-lock block is mandated; only workers get an explicit no-delegation prompt block.
The context capsule timeout_s=90 is very short for a 250-worker deployment, with no timeout handling guidance.
Banned-model fallback detection is required for partial status, but no detection mechanism is specified.
Atomic write requirements are stated, but the .tmp-then-mv pattern is omitted from the commander contract.
retry_budget is declared in context but not wired to workflow steps.
Premium pool, banned-model list, leaf depth lock, 50x5 bounded spawning, and partial/failure conditions are otherwise internally consistent.
